name: Victoria (Australia)

colors.symbolism: the field of the British Blue Ensign
the stars of the Southern Cross, also in the Union Jack
the crosses of the Union Jack
the gold St Edward's Crown above the Southern Cross

description: A blue British ensign with the Union Jack in the canton, the five-star Southern Cross constellation in the fly, and a gold St Edward's Crown above the cross.
history: Adopted in 1877 as a colonial ensign and retained after Australian federation in 1901. The crown above the Southern Cross has changed with the reigning monarch's preferred crown design; the current St Edward's Crown was set in 1953 with the accession of Elizabeth II.

features.symbolism: Based on the British Blue Ensign, the standard format for Australian state flags.
the Union Jack, signifying historic ties to the United Kingdom
the constellation Crux, visible in the southern sky; the stars have 5, 6, 7, 7 and 8 points reflecting their relative brightness

features.role: St Edward's Crown above the Southern Cross
